summary: Full backend + frontend e2e testing of Grewal Engineering Work portal (MVP first run). Backend: 22/23 pytest tests passed. Frontend: all 11 critical UI flows verified (landing, invalid login, admin login, master dispatch manual create, exports visible, module placeholder + integration ping, reports filters, settings 3 tabs, logout, dispatch role hides Settings, protected route redirect). AI extraction via Gemini worked end-to-end with a reportlab-generated invoice PDF and returned parsed entries. Company profile publish/unpublish public endpoint verified. Only one real bug: brute-force lockout does not trigger because the app tracks attempts by request.client.host, which is the Kubernetes ingress internal IP and varies per request (observed 10.79.151.133 and 10.79.151.130 splitting counts).

critical.issue: Brute-force lockout never triggers behind ingress. auth_routes.py uses `request.client.host` for the identifier, but the ingress terminates TLS and forwards from multiple internal IPs, so 6 rapid bad attempts split across different identifiers and never reach the MAX_ATTEMPTS=5 threshold. Confirmed in mongo: same fake username produced 3 separate login_attempts docs across 2 different IPs. Fix: honor X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP header (take first hop), or use username-only identifier as fallback.

minor.issue: GET /api/dispatch (list_entries) has no role check — dispatch role can list all entries, which is expected, but the `user` fixture is unused in list_entries; keeping for consistency is fine.

DispatchEntryInput is used for PUT with all-defaults; a client sending a partial update would blank out fields (e.g., invoice_date, dispatch_id_link fields). Consider PATCH semantics or requiring full payload in the UI.

issues: Radix DialogContent missing aria-describedby / Description — accessibility warning in console. Add <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby to each Dialog.

action_items: Fix brute-force lockout: derive client IP from X-Forwarded-For header (first value) in /api/auth/login instead of request.client.host, OR key attempts on username only. Verify by rerunning TestAuth::test_brute_force_lockout.
Add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby="undefined" removal) to Radix Dialogs in MasterDispatch.jsx and Settings.jsx to remove a11y console warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_routes.py uses request.client.host which is unreliable behind ingress — same issue would affect any per-IP rate limiting.
CORS_ORIGINS defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True which browsers reject; production should set an explicit origin list in backend/.env.
MasterDispatch.jsx is likely large; consider splitting extraction/table/dialogs into subcomponents if it exceeds 700 lines.
seed_user only updates password if changed but never updates name/role — acceptable for MVP.
No pagination on GET /api/dispatch (capped at 1000) — will be a problem at scale.
seed_data_creation: Test dispatch entry UITEST-INV-001 created via UI manual entry (still in DB). TEST_INV_001 was created & deleted via pytest. login_attempts collection has residual records for nonexistent_zzz and lockout_test_zzz — safe to delete but harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is stable and runs in ~14s with pytest-xdist. Only test_brute_force_lockout fails — will pass once main agent honors X-Forwarded-For for the identifier. AI extraction test (TestExtraction::test_extract_invoice_pdf) uses reportlab to synthesize a realistic invoice and calls Gemini via emergentintegrations (takes ~5-10s). Frontend UI flows all pass — no re-run needed for UI unless dialog/table changes.
rca of the issue: Brute-force lockout: In /app/backend/routes/auth_routes.py line 16, identifier=f"{request.client.host}:{username}". Behind the emergent ingress, request.client.host resolves to the pod-level ingress IP which varies per connection (observed 10.79.151.130 and 10.79.151.133). Each request lands on a different identifier, so the counter never reaches MAX_ATTEMPTS=5. Reproduction: POST /api/auth/login 6 times with same fake username + wrong password from an external client — all return 401, none return 429. Mitigation: use `request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for', request.client.host).split(',')[0].strip()` for the IP portion, or key the identifier on username alone (safer for a small internal portal).
